member.name: M:Microsoft.Internal.GamesTest.Rpc.Server.RpcServer.ProcessPendingRequests
member.summary: Synchronously processes requests received since the last call and raises queued events.
member.remarks: If this method is not called repeatedly to process and clear the request queue, hooks will not be called and remote method calls on respective clients will time out. Clients will also stop receiving event notifications for raised events. The request queue may also become full due to continuously arriving requests that aren't dequeued and processed. GAMESTEST_RPC_E_PENDING_REQUESTS_QUEUE_FULL error responses will be sent to all clients until the queue is processed. Attempting to raise events at that time will return the same error code.
member.summary: Synchronously processes all pending requests and raise queued events.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Internal.GamesTest.Rpc.Server.RpcArchiveImpl.SerializeTypeMetadata
member.summary: Serializes continuously recorded meta-data for each field stored in m_fieldTypes map. into the hidden array at the root of Archive's JSON structure.
member.remarks: This is done lazily just before the continuously maintained JSON object for the entire archive is serialized to string.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Internal.GamesTest.Rpc.Server.RpcArchiveImpl.AddTypeMetadataIfMissing
member.summary: Creates a hidden meta-data element that allows JsonContractSerializer to parse the Archive object in C# client.
member.remarks: Also creates a placeholder for a hidden array used to contain meta-data for each named value. This array is not filled in with valid information from m_fieldTypes until the moment that the archive is serialized.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Internal.GamesTest.Rpc.Server.RpcArchiveImpl.GetNamedStringCollection(System.Char!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsConst*,System.Char**,System.UInt32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong,System.Char*,System.UInt32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong)
member.summary: Gets the contents of the collection in the field with given name.
member.param: The name of the field that represents the string collection whose content is to be retrieved.
A pointer to an array of wchar_t pointers that will be used to hold the starting address of each string in the collection referred to by the parameter.
A unsigned long that indicates the total number of elements in the array pointed to by the parameter.
A pointer to an array of wide characters that will be used to hold the entire content of every string in the collection referred to by the parameter.
A unsigned long that indicates the number of wide characters in the array pointed to by the parameter.
member.returns: S_OK if the collection was able to retrieved successfully, otherwise an HRESULT representing a failure.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Internal.GamesTest.Rpc.Server.RpcArchiveImpl.MeasureNamedStringCollection(System.Char!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsConst*,System.UInt32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong*,System.UInt32!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsLong*)
member.summary: Gets both the number of strings in the field with given name as well as the total number of characters contained within the collection.
member.param: The name of the field that represents the string collection whose length is to be retrieved.
A pointer to a unsigned long that will contain the number of strings in the collection indicated by the parameter.
A pointer to a unsigned long that will contain the total number of all characters in the entire string collection. This includes space for null-termination characters.
member.returns: S_OK if the number of strings in the collection and the total number characters in the collection were able to retrieved successfully, otherwise an HRESULT representing a failure.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Internal.GamesTest.Rpc.Server.RpcArchiveImpl.m_fieldTypes
member.summary: Records the original types of all archive fields as they are set.
member.remarks: Used for lazy update of a hidden element in Archive's JSON structure only when serialization is requested. We cannot infer the types of archive fields because they are converted to JSON types on the fly, and there is no 1:1 correlation between source types used in SetNamedValue/Collection and resulting JSON types. Because of this, we have to record the type information on the fly and store it.

member.summary: The underlying JsonObject that contains all archive fields.

member.summary: An implementation of the RpcArchive class.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Internal.GamesTest.Rpc.Server.WinsockListeningSocket.IsListening
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this server has been started.

member.name: M:Concurrency.affinity_partitioner.Dispose
member.summary: Destroys an affinity_partitioner object.

member.name: M:Concurrency.affinity_partitioner.#ctor
member.summary: Constructs an affinity_partitioner object.

member.name: T:Concurrency.affinity_partitioner
member.summary: The affinity_partitioner class is similar to the static_partitioner class, but it improves cache affinity by its choice of mapping subranges to worker threads. It can improve performance significantly when a loop is re-executed over the same data set, and the data fits in cache. Note that the same affinity_partitioner object must be used with subsequent iterations of a parallel loop that is executed over a particular data set, to benefit from data locality.

member.name: M:Concurrency.simple_partitioner.Dispose
member.summary: Destroys a simple_partitioner object.

member.name: M:Concurrency.simple_partitioner.#ctor(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Constructs a simple_partitioner object.
member.param: Number of iterations per chunk.

member.name: T:Concurrency.simple_partitioner
member.summary: The simple_partitioner class represents a static partitioning of the range iterated over by parallel_for. The partitioner divides the range into chunks such that each chunk has at least the number of iterations specified by the chunk size.

member.name: M:Concurrency.static_partitioner.Dispose
member.summary: Destroys a static_partitioner object.

member.name: M:Concurrency.static_partitioner.#ctor
member.summary: Constructs a static_partitioner object.

member.name: T:Concurrency.static_partitioner
member.summary: The static_partitioner class represents a static partitioning of the range iterated over by parallel_for. The partitioner divides the range into as many chunks as there are workers available to the underyling scheduler.

member.name: M:Concurrency.auto_partitioner.Dispose
member.summary: Destroys a auto_partitioner object.

member.name: M:Concurrency.auto_partitioner.#ctor
member.summary: Constructs a auto_partitioner object.

member.name: T:Concurrency.auto_partitioner
member.summary: The auto_partitioner class represents the default method parallel_for, parallel_for_each and parallel_transform use to partition the range they iterates over. This method of partitioning employes range stealing for load balancing as well as per-iterate cancellation.

member.name: M:Concurrency.is_current_task_group_canceling
member.summary: Returns an indication of whether the task group which is currently executing inline on the current context is in the midst of an active cancellation (or will be shortly). Note that if there is no task group currently executing inline on the current context, false will be returned.
member.returns: true if the task group which is currently executing is canceling, false otherwise.
member.remarks: For more information, see .

member.name: M:Concurrency.interruption_point
member.summary: Creates an interruption point for cancellation. If a cancellation is in progress in the context where this function is called, this will throw an internal exception that aborts the execution of the currently executing parallel work. If cancellation is not in progress, the function does nothing.
member.remarks: You should not catch the internal cancellation exception thrown by the interruption_point() function. The exception will be caught and handled by the runtime, and catching it may cause your program to behave abnormally.

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_group.is_canceling
member.summary: Informs the caller whether or not the task group is currently in the midst of a cancellation. This does not necessarily indicate that the cancel method was called on the task_group object (although such certainly qualifies this method to return true). It may be the case that the task_group object is executing inline and a task group further up in the work tree was canceled. In cases such as these where the runtime can determine ahead of time that cancellation will flow through this task_group object, true will be returned as well.
member.returns: An indication of whether the task_group object is in the midst of a cancellation (or is guaranteed to be shortly).
member.remarks: For more information, see .

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_group.cancel
member.summary: Makes a best effort attempt to cancel the sub-tree of work rooted at this task group. Every task scheduled on the task group will get canceled transitively if possible.
member.remarks: For more information, see .

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_group.wait
member.summary: Waits until all work on the task_group object has either completed or been canceled.
member.returns: An indication of whether the wait was satisfied or the task group was canceled, due to either an explicit cancel operation or an exception being thrown from one of its tasks. For more information, see task_group_status.
member.remarks:
Note that one or more of the tasks scheduled to this task_group object may execute inline on the calling context.
If one or more of the tasks scheduled to this task_group object throws an exception, the runtime will select one such exception of its choosing and propagate it out of the call to the wait method.
Calling wait on a task_group object resets it to a clean state where it can be reused. This includes the case where the task_group object was canceled.
In the non-exceptional path of execution, you have a mandate to call either this method or the run_and_wait method before the destructor of the task_group executes.

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_group.Dispose
member.summary: Destroys a task_group object. You are expected to call the either the wait or run_and_wait method on the object prior to the destructor executing, unless the destructor is executing as the result of stack unwinding due to an exception.
member.remarks: If the destructor runs as the result of normal execution (for example, not stack unwinding due to an exception) and neither the wait nor run_and_wait methods have been called, the destructor may throw a missing_wait exception.

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_group.#ctor(Concurrency.cancellation_token)
member.summary: Constructs a new task_group object.
member.param: A cancellation token to associate with this task group. The task group will be canceled when the token is canceled.
member.remarks: The constructor that takes a cancellation token creates a task_group that will be canceled when the source associated with the token is canceled. Providing an explicit cancellation token also isolates this task group from participating in an implicit cancellation from a parent group with a different token or no token.

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_group.#ctor
member.summary: Constructs a new task_group object.
member.remarks: The constructor that takes a cancellation token creates a task_group that will be canceled when the source associated with the token is canceled. Providing an explicit cancellation token also isolates this task group from participating in an implicit cancellation from a parent group with a different token or no token.

member.name: T:Concurrency.task_group
member.summary: The task_group class represents a collection of parallel work which can be waited on or canceled.
member.remarks:
Unlike the heavily restricted structured_task_group class, the task_group class is much more general construct. It does not have any of the restrictions described by structured_task_group. task_group objects may safely be used across threads and utilized in free-form ways. The disadvantage of the task_group construct is that it may not perform as well as the structured_task_group construct for tasks which perform small amounts of work.
For more information, see .

member.name: M:Concurrency.structured_task_group.is_canceling
member.summary: Informs the caller whether or not the task group is currently in the midst of a cancellation. This does not necessarily indicate that the cancel method was called on the structured_task_group object (although such certainly qualifies this method to return true). It may be the case that the structured_task_group object is executing inline and a task group further up in the work tree was canceled. In cases such as these where the runtime can determine ahead of time that cancellation will flow through this structured_task_group object, true will be returned as well.
member.returns: An indication of whether the structured_task_group object is in the midst of a cancellation (or is guaranteed to be shortly).
member.remarks: For more information, see .

member.name: M:Concurrency.structured_task_group.cancel
member.summary: Makes a best effort attempt to cancel the sub-tree of work rooted at this task group. Every task scheduled on the task group will get canceled transitively if possible.
member.remarks: For more information, see .

member.name: M:Concurrency.structured_task_group.wait
member.summary: Waits until all work on the structured_task_group has completed or is canceled.
member.returns: An indication of whether the wait was satisfied or the task group was canceled, due to either an explicit cancel operation or an exception being thrown from one of its tasks. For more information, see task_group_status
member.remarks:
Note that one or more of the tasks scheduled to this structured_task_group object may execute inline on the calling context.
If one or more of the tasks scheduled to this structured_task_group object throws an exception, the runtime will select one such exception of its choosing and propagate it out of the call to the wait method.
After this function returns, the structured_task_group object is considered in a final state and should not be used. Note that utilization after the wait method returns will result in undefined behavior.
In the non-exceptional path of execution, you have a mandate to call either this method or the run_and_wait method before the destructor of the structured_task_group executes.

member.name: M:Concurrency.structured_task_group.Dispose
member.summary: Destroys a structured_task_group object. You are expected to call either the wait or run_and_wait method on the object prior to the destructor executing, unless the destructor is executing as a result of stack unwinding due to an exception.
member.remarks: If the destructor runs as the result of normal execution (for example, not stack unwinding due to an exception) and neither the wait nor run_and_wait methods have been called, the destructor may throw a missing_wait exception.

member.name: M:Concurrency.structured_task_group.#ctor(Concurrency.cancellation_token)
member.summary: Constructs a new structured_task_group object.
member.param: A cancellation token to associate with this structured task group. The structured task group will be canceled when the token is canceled.
member.remarks: The constructor that takes a cancellation token creates a structured_task_group that will be canceled when the source associated with the token is canceled. Providing an explicit cancellation token also isolates this structured task group from participating in an implicit cancellation from a parent group with a different token or no token.

member.name: M:Concurrency.structured_task_group.#ctor
member.summary: Constructs a new structured_task_group object.
member.remarks: The constructor that takes a cancellation token creates a structured_task_group that will be canceled when the source associated with the token is canceled. Providing an explicit cancellation token also isolates this structured task group from participating in an implicit cancellation from a parent group with a different token or no token.

member.name: T:Concurrency.structured_task_group
member.summary: The structured_task_group class represents a highly structured collection of parallel work. You can queue individual parallel tasks to a structured_task_group using task_handle objects, and wait for them to complete, or cancel the task group before they have finished executing, which will abort any tasks that have not begun execution.
member.remarks:
There are a number of severe restrictions placed on usage of a structured_task_group object in order to gain performance:
A single structured_task_group object cannot be used by multiple threads. All operations on a structured_task_group object must be performed by the thread that created the object. The two exceptions to this rule are the member functions cancel and is_canceling. The object may not be in the capture list of a lambda expression and be used within a task, unless the task is using one of the cancellation operations.
All tasks scheduled to a structured_task_group object are scheduled through the use of task_handle objects which you must explicitly manage the lifetime of.
Multiple groups may only be used in absolutely nested order. If two structured_task_group objects are declared, the second one being declared (the inner one) must destruct before any method except cancel or is_canceling is called on the first one (the outer one). This condition holds true in both the case of simply declaring multiple structured_task_group objects within the same or functionally nested scopes as well as the case of a task that was queued to the structured_task_group via the run or run_and_wait methods.
Unlike the general task_group class, all states in the structured_task_group class are final. After you have queued tasks to the group and waited for them to complete, you may not use the same group again.
For more information, see .

member.name: M:Concurrency.op_LogicalOr(Concurrency.task{System.Void}!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsConst*!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsImplicitlyDereferenced,Concurrency.task{System.Void}!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsConst*!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsImplicitlyDereferenced)
member.summary: Creates a task that will complete successfully when any of the tasks supplied as arguments completes successfully.
member.typeparam: The type of the returned task.
member.param: The first task to combine into the resulting task.
The second task to combine into the resulting task.
member.returns:
A task that completes successfully when either of the input tasks has completed successfully. If the input tasks are of type T, the output of this function will be a task<std::vector<T>. If the input tasks are of type void the output task will also be a task<void>.
To allow for a construct of the sort taskA || taskB && taskC, which are combined in pairs, with && taking precedence over ||, the operator|| produces a task<std::vector<T>> if one of the tasks is of type task<std::vector<T>> and the other one is of type task<T>.
member.remarks: If both of the tasks are canceled or throw exceptions, the returned task will complete in the canceled state, and one of the exceptions, if any are encountered, will be thrown when you call get() or wait() on that task.

member.name: M:Concurrency.op_LogicalAnd(Concurrency.task{System.Void}!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsConst*!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsImplicitlyDereferenced,Concurrency.task{System.Void}!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsConst*!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsImplicitlyDereferenced)
member.summary: Creates a task that will complete successfully when both of the tasks supplied as arguments complete successfully.
member.typeparam: The type of the returned task.
member.param: The first task to combine into the resulting task.
The second task to combine into the resulting task.
member.returns:
A task that completes successfully when both of the input tasks have completed successfully. If the input tasks are of type T, the output of this function will be a task<std::vector<T>>. If the input tasks are of type void the output task will also be a task<void>.
To allow for a construct of the sort taskA && taskB && taskC, which are combined in pairs, the && operator produces a task<std::vector<T>> if either one or both of the tasks are of type task<std::vector<T>>.
member.remarks: If one of the tasks is canceled or throws an exception, the returned task will complete early, in the canceled state, and the exception, if one is encountered, will be thrown if you call get() or wait() on that task.

member.name: M:Concurrency.details._Task_impl_base._CancelAndRunContinuations(System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,std.shared_ptr<Concurrency.details._ExceptionHolder>!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsConst*!System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsImplicitlyDereferenced)
member.summary: Requests cancellation on the task and schedules continuations if the task can be transitioned to a terminal state.
member.param: Set to true if the cancel takes place as a result of the task body encountering an exception, or because an ancestor or task_completion_event the task was registered with were canceled with an exception. A synchronous cancel is one that assures the task could not be running on a different thread at the time the cancellation is in progress. An asynchronous cancel is one where the thread performing the cancel has no control over the thread that could be executing the task, that is the task could execute concurrently while the cancellation is in progress.
Whether an exception other than the internal runtime cancellation exceptions caused this cancellation.
Whether this exception came from an ancestor task or a task_completion_event as opposed to an exception that was encountered by the task itself. Only valid when _UserException is set to true.
The exception holder that represents the exception. Only valid when _UserException is set to true.

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_continuation_context.use_synchronous_execution
member.summary: Returns a task continuation context object that represents the synchronous execution context.
member.returns: The synchronous execution context.

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_continuation_context.get_current_winrt_context
member.summary: Returns a task continuation context object that represents the current winrt thread context.
member.returns: The current winrt thread context.
member.remarks:
This method captures the current caller's Windows Runtime thread context. It is very similar to what task_continuation_context::use_current() is doing except it is also available without C++/Cx extension support. Please approach this method with caution as it is designed for advanced users writing C++/Cx agnostic library code. task_continuation_context::use_current() will give you more protection from mistakes.
This method is only available to Windows Store apps.

member.name: M:Concurrency.task_continuation_context.use_default
member.summary: Creates the default task continuation context.
member.returns: The default continuation context.
member.remarks:
The default context is used if you don't specify a continuation context when you call the then method. In Windows applications for Windows 7 and below, as well as desktop applications on Windows 8 and higher, the runtime determines where task continuations will execute. However, in a Windows Store app, the default continuation context for a continuation on an apartment aware task is the apartment where then is invoked.
An apartment aware task is a task that unwraps a Windows Runtime IAsyncInfo interface, or a task that is descended from such a task. Therefore, if you schedule a continuation on an apartment aware task in a Windows Runtime STA, the continuation will execute in that STA.
A continuation on a non-apartment aware task will execute in a context the Runtime chooses.

member.name: T:Concurrency.task_continuation_context
member.summary: The task_continuation_context class allows you to specify where you would like a continuation to be executed. It is only useful to use this class from a Windows Store app. For non-Windows Store apps, the task continuation's execution context is determined by the runtime, and not configurable.

member.summary: Schedule a functor with automatic inlining. Note that this is "fire and forget" scheduling, which cannot be waited on or canceled after scheduling. This schedule method will perform automatic inlining base on .
member.param: The user functor need to be scheduled.

member.name: T:Concurrency.details._TaskProcThunk
member.summary: Helper object used for LWT invocation.

member.name: T:Concurrency.details._TaskCreationCallstack
member.summary: Callstack container, which is used to capture and preserve callstacks in ppltasks. Members of this class is examined by vc debugger, thus there will be no public access methods. Please note that names of this class should be kept stable for debugger examining.

member.name: M:Concurrency.details._DefaultTaskHelper._NoCallOnDefaultTask_ErrorImpl
member.summary: Helper function for throwing an error for task functions that cannot be called from a default constructed task.

member.name: M:Concurrency.cancel_current_task
member.summary: Helper macro to determine how many stack frames need to be saved. When any number less or equal to 1 is specified, only one frame is captured and no stackwalk will be involved. Otherwise, the number of callstack frames will be captured.
member.remarks: This needs to be defined as a macro rather than a function so that if we're only gathering one frame, _ReturnAddress() will evaluate to client code, rather than a helper function inside of _TaskCreationCallstack, itself.
member.summary:
Cancels the currently executing task. This function can be called from within the body of a task to abort the task's execution and cause it to enter the canceled state. While it may be used in response to a cancellation request through a cancellation_token, you may also use it by itself, to initiate cancellation of the task that is currently executing.
It is not a supported scenario to call this function if you are not within the body of a task. Doing so will result in undefined behavior such as a crash or a hang in your application.

member.name: D:Concurrency.task_status
member.summary: A type that represents the terminal state of a task. Valid values are completed and canceled.
